rationale: The citing paper explicitly positions itself as extending prior marketing-finance work on how advertising affects stock-market outcomes, and the cited paper is directly in that stream (advertising effects plus shareholder value and value volatility in a longitudinal panel). Given this strong construct overlap (advertising, shareholder value/stock outcomes, risk/volatility) and a DOI-confirmed reference match, this is plausibly more than an incidental mention and should be reviewed in full.
